manual.

sections.question_synthetic: What are the threshold statement filing obligations for a ballot measure committee under Government Code Section 84200(b)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Ballot measure committees like the Coalition must file campaign statements for threshold periods if they make expenditures of $500 or more or make/receive contributions of $500 or more during a threshold period, as they do not qualify for the exemption in Section 84200(b)(4) which applies only to candidate and elected officer committees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers; extracted question and conclusion from letter body content. Letter confirms prior telephone conversation and clarifies discrepancy between statute and information manual. Some OCR artifacts present in document text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the threshold statement filing obligations for a ballot measure committee under Government Code Section 84200(b)?

CONCLUSION: Ballot measure committees like the Coalition must file campaign statements for threshold periods if they make expenditures of $500 or more or make/receive contributions of $500 or more during a threshold period, as they do not qualify for the exemption in Section 84200(b)(4) which applies only to candidate and elected officer committees.

As I indicated to you in our telephone conversation, there is a discrepancy between the requirements contained in Government Code Section 84200(b) and the informati0n contained in the "1981 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Pro- visions of the Political Reform Act." It is the Commission's policy that in the event such a discrepancy occurs, a person who has received a correct interpretation of the law from the Commission's staff is required to comply with the staff's advice, rather than with the information contained in the manual. Government Code Section 84200(b) states that in addition to the semi-annual campaign statements due on July 31 and J a nu'a r y 31, w hi c h are r e qui red if the r e has bee n ~ act i v it Y during the half-year covered, campaign statements reporting activity during a threshold period may also be required. Elected officers. candidates, and their controlled committees, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose a candi- date, are excepted from this required reporting under Section 84200(b)(4) which states that: Notwithstanding the above, elected officers, candidates, and their controlled committees and committees formed primarily to support or oppose a candidate whose activities within a threshold period consist only of receiving contributions and making expenditures solely in connecti n with their own or their candidates amp igns ar~ not required to file campaign statements pursuant to this subdivision during any six-month period (frum Kelly Lmtf 1 Jan u a r y 1 to J u n e 3 0 r: r f r CJ'1: J I: lIt Decem~er 31) in ~hich th candidat IS office is not to be voted on .... Since the Coalition is a co~mittee formed primarily to support or oppose a ballot measure, it does not come under the previsions for committee described under Se tion 84200 (b) (4). Therefore. the Coalition must fl Ie camp3 gn state- ments for the threshold periods if it has met the single criterion--making expenditures of $500 or more or m king or receiving contributions of $500 or morL during threshold I period. Please contact me if you have any ad~~tional questions. Sincerely, Jeanne Pritchard Political Reform C nsultant
embedding.summary: FPPC letter to Kelly Kimball of California Coalition to Stop the Peripheral Canal clarifying threshold statement filing requirements under Government Code Section 84200(b), confirming staff telephone advice that ballot measure committees must file if they meet the $500 expenditure or contribution threshold.
